title: Thin Scope, Vertical Slices, and Evidence-Driven Roadmap · Changed-Stakeholder Lab
summary: An independent public-benefit team supplies different users, constraints, risks, technology, data, and failure conditions and transfers thin scope, vertical slices, and evidence-driven roadmap into a changed risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.
Slice work across interface, behavior, data, verification, accessibility, security, and operations to produce one end-to-end learning signal.
Order slices by prerequisite knowledge, risk retirement, feedback value, and reversible commitment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Story Outcome
steps.instruction:
lab case — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The lab variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Story Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Story Outcome, keep Story Outcome connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The lab variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Story Outcome condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Secure Software Development Framework — Controls prepared organizations, protected software, well-secured production, vulnerability response, provenance, verification, and release evidence.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Lab Story Outcome, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Story Outcome precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Lab Story Outcome, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The lab variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

For Commit to the mission model · Lab Story Outcome, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A component or database task is automatically a user story.

options.text: State the Story Outcome preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
A component or database task is automatically a user story. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

lines.label: observed

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Scope Tradeoff condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Scope Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Scope Tradeoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The lab variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Scope Tradeoff.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Scope Tradeoff, keep Scope Tradeoff connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.
